channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Early primates may have evolved in cold climates, not tropics
item.description: A new study suggests the first primates emerged in cold, dry regions of North America rather than tropical forests, and some may have hibernated.

item.title: Animals use sophisticated communication to cooperate across species
item.description: Animals from different species rely on complex signals to collaborate on tasks like finding food, grooming, or guarding against predators.

item.title: A DNA repair gene turned harmful and revealed a cancer vulnerability
item.description: Researchers found that a gene typically protecting DNA can become dangerous when overproduced, exposing a weakness in cancer cells.

item.title: Study reveals a Wordle strategy that wins 99% of the time
item.description: A new Wordle-solving strategy achieves a 99% success rate by prioritizing information gain over likely answers.
